mnemonics: G.I.F.T.S - Groups Involve Four Things: Identity, Inverses, Closure, and Associativity on a Set
keyPoints: A group is a set combined with an operation that satisfies closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
The identity element is unique for a given group.
Every element in a group has a unique inverse.
commonMistakes: Assuming commutativity (that a * b = b * a always holds); this is only true for Abelian groups.
Forgetting to check the closure property for a subset when determining if it is a subgroup.
